html:
Gaotu Techedu, 18WA.F / GOTU

Gaotu is mispriced, but not for the sloppy reason in the older thesis. It is not yet GAAP profitable. The real setup is better and more nuanced: a post-crackdown Chinese education survivor with positive operating cash flow, sharply narrowing losses, a large liquid balance sheet, active buybacks, and a 2026 pivot from growth-at-all-costs to profit conversion.

At about USD 1.88 per ADS today, equivalent to roughly EUR 1.62 for 18WA.F, the equity value is around USD 450 million. Gaotu ended 2025 with RMB 3.97 billion, or about USD 568 million, of cash, restricted cash, and short- and long-term investments, against only RMB 132 million of borrowings. That does not make the stock risk-free, because deferred revenue and China/VIE governance matter, but it means the market is paying very little for a business that generated RMB 6.15 billion of 2025 revenue, RMB 416 million of operating cash flow, and bought back 12.8% of outstanding shares under its repurchase programs.

The market still labels Gaotu as a busted K-12 tutoring ADR. That label is stale. The current business is a mix of traditional learning services, non-academic tutoring, college/adult education, offline learning centers, and AI-enhanced digital learning. In 2025, revenue grew 35%, gross billings grew 23%, and the operating loss narrowed 57%. Q4 revenue grew 21%, while the net loss narrowed 38%. Deferred revenue rose 23% to RMB 2.57 billion, which matters because it gives visibility into future recognized revenue.

The key forward question is not whether 2025 was profitable. It was not. The question is whether 2026 converts the rebuilt revenue base into earnings. Management is explicitly shifting to “profitable growth.” User acquisition efficiency improved 10.8% in 2025, operating leverage has improved for five consecutive quarters, and several business lines are already profitable at the segment level. Non-academic online/offline initiatives grew revenue 90% for the full year. Traditional learning services grew nearly 15%. College and adult education delivered mid-double-digit revenue and billings growth and reached business-line profitability. Offline learning centers remain a drag, but management expects school-level profitability in 2026 and full offline profitability including headquarters overhead in 2027.

This is the variant perception: Gaotu is not a ban-reversal bet. China crushed hopes of a full after-school tutoring comeback again in early 2026, and that is actually clarifying. The upside case rests on the legal, less politically toxic parts of the education market: adult exams, non-academic learning, AI literacy, offline enrichment, and full-lifecycle learner monetization. China’s April 2026 “AI + Education” policy push is a real tailwind for the parts of Gaotu now trying to differentiate around AI-assisted teaching, personalization, and operational efficiency.

The setup is also uncrowded. Retail chatter is tiny and confused, mostly “why does no one want this?” posts, automated filing summaries, and old GSX-era fraud/litigation reminders. That is exactly the kind of perception overhang that can keep a small ADR cheap after the fundamentals have already turned.

The near-term catalyst is Q1 2026 results on June 2. Guidance is only RMB 1.58-1.60 billion of revenue, up 5.7% to 7.0%, so the headline growth deceleration may scare weak holders. The better read will be gross billings, deferred revenue, marketing efficiency, cash balance, buyback pace, and whether management reaffirms double-digit Q2 growth and 2026 profit conversion. If Q1 looks seasonally soft but cash flow and deferred revenue remain healthy, the stock may be setting up for a second-half re-rating.

The valuation is asymmetric. On a harsh view, haircut the cash, assume slower growth, and give the operating business little credit. The stock is still supported by liquidity and buybacks. On a normalizing view, 2026 revenue near RMB 7.0 billion with modest positive net income and higher free cash flow should not trade below cash. On a bull view, Gaotu becomes a 7% to 10% operating margin education platform on RMB 7.5-8.0 billion of revenue, with hundreds of millions of dollars of cash still available for repurchases or dividends.

The bear case is regulatory relapse, poor capital allocation, offline losses lasting longer than promised, or evidence that 2025 cash flow was mostly deferred-revenue timing rather than durable unit economics. The governance discount is real, and the Frankfurt line is illiquid. But the stock is already priced as if the business is low quality and the cash deserves a deep discount. The upside comes from proving merely that Gaotu is a viable, cash-generative education platform.

Targets from roughly EUR 1.62 current price

Bear: EUR 0.85, down 48%. Q1/Q2 growth fades, offline losses persist, and investors apply a severe China ADR and cash-access discount.

Base: EUR 3.40, up 110%. Revenue grows low-to-mid teens in 2026, consolidated profitability arrives, buybacks continue, and the stock re-rates toward cash plus a modest value for the operating business.

Bull: EUR 6.00, up 270%. AI and offline scale drive real margin expansion, 2027 earnings power approaches USD 90-110 million, and the market values Gaotu as a cash-rich, profitable education compounder rather than a failed tutoring relic.
